basic_details.job_title: BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026: Apply Online for 150 Bihar Police Sub-Inspector Posts
basic_details.department: Home Department (Police Branch), Special Branch, Government of Bihar
basic_details.selection_process_brief: Preliminary Exam, Main Exam, Physical Efficiency Test (PET)
basic_details.short_information: The Bihar Police Subordinate Services Commission (BPSSC) has released the BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026 notification (Advt. No. 09/2026) for direct recruitment to 150 posts of Police Sub-Inspector (General Closed Cadre) under the Special Branch of the Home Department (Police Branch), Government of Bihar. Online applications open on 09/07/2026 and close on 09/08/2026. Selection will be through a Preliminary Exam, Main Exam and Physical Efficiency Test (PET). The application fee is Rs. 100/- for all categories, and the cut-off date for age and qualification is 01/08/2025.

application_fee.details: The application fee is the same for all category male and female candidates and must be paid online. | Application Fee: Rs. 100/- (for all categories) Accepted payment modes: Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card and UPI Bank charges applicable on Net Banking/Credit Card/Debit Card transactions must be borne by the candidate Application fee once paid successfully will not be refunded

age_limit.details: The age limit is calculated as on the cut-off date of 01-08-2025, based on the date of birth recorded in the Matriculation or equivalent certificate. The category-wise age limits are given below. | Category Minimum Age Maximum Age Unreserved (General) - Male 20 Years 37 Years Unreserved (General) - Female 20 Years 40 Years Backward Class / Extremely Backward Class - Male and Female 20 Years 40 Years Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe - Male and Female 20 Years 42 Years | Additional age relaxation is available for specific categories: | Bihar government servants seeking promotion to a higher pay scale/cadre, with a minimum of 3 years of regular service, are eligible for a 5-year relaxation in the maximum age limit. Ex-servicemen applying for civil posts get a relaxation of 3 years plus the length of their defence service, provided their actual age does not exceed 57 years as on the cut-off date.

qualification.details: Both male and female Indian citizens are eligible to apply for the BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026, subject to the educational qualification, age limit and physical standards prescribed in the notification. | Candidates must hold a graduate degree or an equivalent qualification as on 01-08-2025 to be eligible for the Police Sub-Inspector (General Closed Cadre) post. | The age limit is calculated as on the cut-off date of 01-08-2025, based on the date of birth recorded in the Matriculation or equivalent certificate. The category-wise age limits are given below. | Additional age relaxation is available for specific categories: | Bihar government servants seeking promotion to a higher pay scale/cadre, with a minimum of 3 years of regular service, are eligible for a 5-year relaxation in the maximum age limit. Ex-servicemen applying for civil posts get a relaxation of 3 years plus the length of their defence service, provided their actual age does not exceed 57 years as on the cut-off date. | No marks are awarded for height, chest or weight, but candidates who do not meet the prescribed physical standards will be declared unsuccessful at the Physical Efficiency Test stage. | A minimum expansion of 5 cm in the chest is mandatory for male candidates. Chest measurement is not applicable for female candidates. Female candidates of all categories must have a minimum weight of 48 kg.

salary_details.details: The post of Police Sub-Inspector (General Closed Cadre) carries Pay Level-6 as per the notification. The official notification does not mention a specific in-hand salary figure.

selection_process: Paper Type Subjects and Marks Duration Total Marks

Paper I Objective, Multiple-Choice General Studies & Current Affairs (50), Mathematics & Quantitative Aptitude (40), Reasoning & Analytical Ability (40), English Language (20), Hindi Language (20), General Knowledge of Bihar (30) 3 Hours 200
Paper II Subjective/Written Hindi Essay (15), English Essay (15), English Precis (10), Hindi to English Translation (10) 1 Hour 50
Selection to the post of Police Sub-Inspector (General Closed Cadre) will be made through a three-stage process consisting of a Preliminary Exam
a Main Exam and a Physical Efficiency Test (PET).
The Preliminary Exam will consist of objective
multiple-choice questions carrying 200 marks in total.
Number of Questions: 100 Duration: 2 hours Marking: 2 marks for each correct answer Subjects: English
General Knowledge
Hindi Language Knowledge

Reasoning and Current Affairs Candidates scoring less than 30% marks will not be considered for the Main Exam Candidates equal to 10 times the number of vacancies (category-wise merit) will be shortlisted for the Main Exam
based on Preliminary Exam scores The exam may be conducted in multiple shifts
with scores normalized using the Equi-Percentile Method
Candidates who qualify the Preliminary Exam will appear for the Main Exam
which consists of two papers.
Paper I carries 200 questions for 200 marks
with 1 mark for each correct answer and a negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer. A minimum of 30% marks in each paper is mandatory to qualify. Based on Main Exam marks
candidates equal to 3 times the number of vacancies (category-wise merit) will be shortlisted for the Physical Efficiency Test.
Candidates who qualify the Main Exam will undergo verification of height
chest and weight as per the physical standards specified in the notification. No marks are awarded for this stage; it is qualifying in nature
and candidates not meeting the criteria will be declared unsuccessful.
The final merit list will be prepared category-wise
based on marks obtained in the Main Written Exam. In case of a tie in marks
preference will be given to the candidate who is older in age. If the age is also the same
preference will be based on higher educational qualification
and thereafter on the alphabetical order of the name (in Hindi) as recorded in the Matriculation certificate.
Before final appointment
selected candidates will undergo a medical examination along with character and antecedent verification. The medical examination will check vision
color blindness
hearing ability
stammering
flat-foot and knock-knee conditions

among others. Candidates with stammering or lisping defects will be declared ineligible. Candidates with a pending criminal case or a court conviction will also be declared ineligible. Any adverse report or concealment of facts by the candidate will lead to disqualification.
how_to_apply: Candidates can apply for BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026 only through the official BPSSC website within the application window.
Visit the official website of the Bihar Police Subordinate Services Commission: bpssc.bihar.gov.in.
Go to the "Bihar Police" tab and click on the link related to Advt. No. 09/2026.
Complete Part 1 of the online application: registration with name, nationality, mobile number, email ID, Bihar domicile status, reservation category, gender and date of birth.
Complete the fee payment. A registration number and password will be sent via SMS and email after successful registration.
Log in using the registration number and password to complete Part 2 of the application: father's/husband's name, mother's name, address and educational qualification.
Upload the scanned photograph and signature as per the specified format and size.
Review all details using the "Edit Details" option before final submission, as no changes are allowed after the application is confirmed and submitted.
Take a printout of the acknowledgement on A4 size paper after final submission and keep it safely for future reference.
Note: A candidate can submit only one online application. A single mobile number can be used for only one application. Applications sent by post or any other offline mode will not be considered.
important_instructions: The last date for submitting the online application is fixed and will not be extended under any circumstances. Requests for extension of time at any stage of the selection process (written exam, PET, document scrutiny, etc.) will not be entertained for any reason, including family, personal, medical, fracture or pregnancy-related circumstances. Candidates from states other than Bihar claiming a reservation category will be treated as Unreserved (General) category candidates and must apply accordingly; failure to do so will render them ineligible. The 35% horizontal reservation for women is applicable only to female candidates who are original residents (domicile) of Bihar. Candidates should not make multiple payments for a single application. Application fee will not be refunded once payment is successful. In case of failed transactions due to power or internet failure, the amount will be refunded by the payment gateway within 7 working days. Providing false or incorrect reservation category, wrong date of birth, wrong gender, or any false information in the application will lead to rejection of candidature and may invite legal action. It is not necessary to send the filled application to the Commission, but the registration number must be retained for future correspondence. The entire selection process is expected to take approximately 6 months to complete. Candidates should keep their registered mobile number and email ID active throughout the recruitment process, as all important communication will be sent through these channels.

rows.details: Home Department (Police Branch), Special Branch, Government of Bihar
Preliminary Exam, Main Exam, Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

content_sections.heading: Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates must apply within the fixed application window. The Commission has clarified that no extension will be given.

rows.event: Cut-off Date for Age and Qualification

content_sections.heading: BPSSC SI Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: A total of 150 vacancies are notified for the post of Police Sub-Inspector (General Closed Cadre), based on the requisition received from the General Administration Department, Government of Bihar, dated 03-02-2026. The category-wise breakup, including vacancies reserved for women within each category, is given below.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Both male and female Indian citizens are eligible to apply for the BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026, subject to the educational qualification, age limit and physical standards prescribed in the notification. Candidates must hold a graduate degree or an equivalent qualification as on 01-08-2025 to be eligible for the Police Sub-Inspector (General Closed Cadre) post. The age limit is calculated as on the cut-off date of 01-08-2025, based on the date of birth recorded in the Matriculation or equivalent certificate. The category-wise age limits are given below. Additional age relaxation is available for specific categories: Bihar government servants seeking promotion to a higher pay scale/cadre, with a minimum of 3 years of regular service, are eligible for a 5-year relaxation in the maximum age limit. Ex-servicemen applying for civil posts get a relaxation of 3 years plus the length of their defence service, provided their actual age does not exceed 57 years as on the cut-off date. No marks are awarded for height, chest or weight, but candidates who do not meet the prescribed physical standards will be declared unsuccessful at the Physical Efficiency Test stage. A minimum expansion of 5 cm in the chest is mandatory for male candidates. Chest measurement is not applicable for female candidates. Female candidates of all categories must have a minimum weight of 48 kg.

rows.category: Backward Class / Extremely Backward Class - Male and Female
Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe - Male and Female
Extremely Backward Class, SC, ST - Male

content_sections.heading: Pay Scale
content_sections.text: The post of Police Sub-Inspector (General Closed Cadre) carries Pay Level-6 as per the notification. The official notification does not mention a specific in-hand salary figure.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: The application fee is the same for all category male and female candidates and must be paid online. Application Fee: Rs. 100/- (for all categories) Accepted payment modes: Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card and UPI Bank charges applicable on Net Banking/Credit Card/Debit Card transactions must be borne by the candidate Application fee once paid successfully will not be refunded

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection to the post of Police Sub-Inspector (General Closed Cadre) will be made through a three-stage process consisting of a Preliminary Exam, a Main Exam and a Physical Efficiency Test (PET). The Preliminary Exam will consist of objective, multiple-choice questions carrying 200 marks in total. Number of Questions: 100 Duration: 2 hours Marking: 2 marks for each correct answer Subjects: English, General Knowledge, Hindi Language Knowledge, Reasoning and Current Affairs Candidates scoring less than 30% marks will not be considered for the Main Exam Candidates equal to 10 times the number of vacancies (category-wise merit) will be shortlisted for the Main Exam, based on Preliminary Exam scores The exam may be conducted in multiple shifts, with scores normalized using the Equi-Percentile Method Candidates who qualify the Preliminary Exam will appear for the Main Exam, which consists of two papers. Paper I carries 200 questions for 200 marks, with 1 mark for each correct answer and a negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer. A minimum of 30% marks in each paper is mandatory to qualify. Based on Main Exam marks, candidates equal to 3 times the number of vacancies (category-wise merit) will be shortlisted for the Physical Efficiency Test. Candidates who qualify the Main Exam will undergo verification of height, chest and weight as per the physical standards specified in the notification. No marks are awarded for this stage; it is qualifying in nature, and candidates not meeting the criteria will be declared unsuccessful. The final merit list will be prepared category-wise, based on marks obtained in the Main Written Exam. In case of a tie in marks, preference will be given to the candidate who is older in age. If the age is also the same, preference will be based on higher educational qualification, and thereafter on the alphabetical order of the name (in Hindi) as recorded in the Matriculation certificate. Before final appointment, selected candidates will undergo a medical examination along with character and antecedent verification. The medical examination will check vision, color blindness, hearing ability, stammering, flat-foot and knock-knee conditions, among others. Candidates with stammering or lisping defects will be declared ineligible. Candidates with a pending criminal case or a court conviction will also be declared ineligible. Any adverse report or concealment of facts by the candidate will lead to disqualification.

rows.subjects_and_marks: General Studies & Current Affairs (50), Mathematics & Quantitative Aptitude (40), Reasoning & Analytical Ability (40), English Language (20), Hindi Language (20), General Knowledge of Bihar (30)
Hindi Essay (15), English Essay (15), English Precis (10), Hindi to English Translation (10)

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Candidates should keep the following ready during the online application and later stages of the selection process: Matriculation or equivalent certificate (for date of birth verification) Educational qualification certificates (graduate or equivalent) Category/caste certificate, where applicable Bihar domicile certificate, where applicable for reservation benefit Income and asset certificate for EWS candidates (valid for one year from date of issue, based on financial year 2025-26 income) Recent color passport-size photograph (2 months old, white background, 15-25 KB, JPG/JPEG/GIF format) Scanned signature in English and Hindi (black or blue ink, white background, 15-25 KB, JPG/JPEG/GIF format) Printed copy of the final submitted application acknowledgement

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Online for BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates can apply for BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026 only through the official BPSSC website within the application window. Visit the official website of the Bihar Police Subordinate Services Commission: bpssc.bihar.gov.in. Go to the "Bihar Police" tab and click on the link related to Advt. No. 09/2026. Complete Part 1 of the online application: registration with name, nationality, mobile number, email ID, Bihar domicile status, reservation category, gender and date of birth. Complete the fee payment. A registration number and password will be sent via SMS and email after successful registration. Log in using the registration number and password to complete Part 2 of the application: father's/husband's name, mother's name, address and educational qualification. Upload the scanned photograph and signature as per the specified format and size. Review all details using the "Edit Details" option before final submission, as no changes are allowed after the application is confirmed and submitted. Take a printout of the acknowledgement on A4 size paper after final submission and keep it safely for future reference. Note: A candidate can submit only one online application. A single mobile number can be used for only one application. Applications sent by post or any other offline mode will not be considered.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: The last date for submitting the online application is fixed and will not be extended under any circumstances. Requests for extension of time at any stage of the selection process (written exam, PET, document scrutiny, etc.) will not be entertained for any reason, including family, personal, medical, fracture or pregnancy-related circumstances. Candidates from states other than Bihar claiming a reservation category will be treated as Unreserved (General) category candidates and must apply accordingly; failure to do so will render them ineligible. The 35% horizontal reservation for women is applicable only to female candidates who are original residents (domicile) of Bihar. Candidates should not make multiple payments for a single application. Application fee will not be refunded once payment is successful. In case of failed transactions due to power or internet failure, the amount will be refunded by the payment gateway within 7 working days. Providing false or incorrect reservation category, wrong date of birth, wrong gender, or any false information in the application will lead to rejection of candidature and may invite legal action. It is not necessary to send the filled application to the Commission, but the registration number must be retained for future correspondence. The entire selection process is expected to take approximately 6 months to complete. Candidates should keep their registered mobile number and email ID active throughout the recruitment process, as all important communication will be sent through these channels.

faq.question: Q1. What is the BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026 notification about?
faq.answer: The Bihar Police Subordinate Services Commission (BPSSC) has released Advt. No. 09/2026 for direct recruitment to 150 posts of Police Sub-Inspector (General Closed Cadre) under the Special Branch of the Home Department (Police Branch), Government of Bihar.

faq.question: Q2. When can I apply for BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application window opens on 09/07/2026 and closes on 09/08/2026. The last date will not be extended.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are there in BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 150 vacancies are notified for the post of Police Sub-Inspector (General Closed Cadre), across Unreserved, EWS, EBC, SC, BC and ST categories.

faq.question: Q4. What is the educational qualification for BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold a graduate degree or an equivalent qualification as on 01-08-2025.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit ranges from 20 to 37 years for Unreserved male candidates, 20 to 40 years for Unreserved female and Backward/Extremely Backward Class candidates, and 20 to 42 years for SC/ST candidates, as on the cut-off date of 01-08-2025.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is Rs. 100/- for all category male and female candidates, payable online.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection consists of a Preliminary Exam, a Main Exam (two papers) and a Physical Efficiency Test (PET), followed by document verification and a medical examination.

faq.question: Q8. What are the physical standards required for BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum height requirement is 165 cm for Unreserved/Backward Class males, 160 cm for EBC/SC/ST males, and 155 cm for all category females. Male candidates must also meet minimum chest measurement criteria, and female candidates must weigh at least 48 kg.

faq.question: Q9. How long will the BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026 selection process take?
faq.answer: The Commission states that the selection process is generally expected to take about 6 months to complete.

faq.question: Q10. How do I apply for BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply online through bpssc.bihar.gov.in under the "Bihar Police" tab, complete both parts of the registration form, upload photograph and signature, pay the application fee, and submit the form before the last date.
